vulnerabilities.description: CVE-2022-0839 is an XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability in Liquibase Core versions prior to 4.8.0 via the SAXParser. An attacker could exploit this to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery (SSRF) through crafted XML changelog files.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase Secure 4.8.0+ includes the fix. All current Liquibase Secure distributions are built from source with the patched SAXParser configuration.
vulnerabilities.workaround: If using Liquibase < 4.8.0, disable XML changelog parsing or upgrade to 4.8.0+. Alternatively, ensure all changelog files come from trusted sources only.

ratings.justification: Code path containing the vulnerability is not present in Liquibase Secure builds. NVD score reflects theoretical maximum; contextual score reflects actual exploitability.

analysis.detail: XXE vulnerability fixed in Liquibase 4.8.0. Our liquibase-core is built from source with the fix included. Scanner incorrectly matches the non-semantic version format (master-<sha>).
False CPE match. liquibase-commercial-mongodb is a Liquibase extension, not MongoDB server. The vulnerability applies to MongoDB server only.

GraalVM Python setuptools path traversal vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process package inputs; no package installation or processing occurs at runtime.

vulnerabilities.description: Netty HttpObjectDecoder incorrectly terminates chunk header parsing at \r\n inside quoted strings in chunked transfer encoding extensions, enabling HTTP request smuggling.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase does not run an HTTP server.

ratings.justification: Server-side HTTP vulnerability. Liquibase never accepts inbound HTTP connections; Netty is only used as a client transport.

analysis.detail: HTTP request smuggling via chunked transfer encoding in Netty HTTP codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/1.1 requests to a Netty HTTP server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport for outbound connections. There is no listening socket for an attacker to exploit.

vulnerabilities.description: Netty verifyContinuationFrame fails to limit the number of zero-byte CONTINUATION frames, bypassing maxHeaderListSize and causing excessive CPU consumption on the server.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase does not run an HTTP/2 server.

ratings.justification: Server-side HTTP/2 vulnerability. Liquibase never accepts inbound HTTP/2 connections; Netty is only used as a client transport.

analysis.detail: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood DoS in Netty HTTP/2 codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport. There is no inbound HTTP/2 listener.

vulnerabilities.description: Jackson Core 3.x StreamReadConstraints.maxDocumentLength check can be bypassed in multiple code paths, weakening DoS protection for applications that rely on it.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase does not rely on maxDocumentLength for protection.

ratings.justification: Liquibase does not use StreamReadConstraints.maxDocumentLength and does not process untrusted JSON input.

analysis.detail: Document length constraint bypass in Jackson Core 3.x. The StreamReadConstraints.maxDocumentLength check can be bypassed in blocking parsers, async parsers, and DataInput path. jackson-core 3.x is a transitive dependency from Spring Boot 4.0.3 in liquibase-license-utility. Liquibase does not set maxDocumentLength (zero usages in codebase), does not process untrusted JSON input from external sources, and is a CLI tool — not a network-facing service. The constraint bypass only matters if relied upon for DoS protection, which Liquibase does not.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.
False CPE match. liquibase-license-utility is not liquibase-core. The vulnerability only affects liquibase-core versions < 4.8.0.

GnuPG vulnerability in OS packages (dirmngr, gnupg, gpg, gpg-agent, gpgconf, gpgsm, gpgv, keyboxd, gnupg-utils). GnuPG is installed in the Docker image for package signature verification during build but is not invoked at runtime by Liquibase. The Liquibase application does not call any GnuPG binaries.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

urllib3 cookie header leak vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. No HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

sqlparse ReDoS vulnerability. sqlparse is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its SQL parsing functionality is never invoked at runtime. Liquibase uses its own Java SQL parser; the Python sqlparse module is never called.

Netty SslHandler resource exhaustion vulnerability via SSLEngine. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious SSL/TLS data to a Netty server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run a TLS server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport for outbound connections.

Netty HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame validation bypass in codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.10.0. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.12.2. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.
Fixed in msal4j 1.15.1. Liquibase Secure uses msal4j 1.23.1, which includes the fix.
False CPE match. cassandra-driver-kerberos is not the Authenticator 2FA app. The vulnerability applies to the Authenticator mobile application.

False positive due to classifier confusion. Trivy reads the JAR's internal pom.properties which reports version 13.2.1 (without the jre11 classifier), causing a false version comparison. The installed JAR is 13.2.1.jre11 which contains the fix. Microsoft's non-standard Maven versioning uses a classifier suffix instead of a standard version bump.

GraalVM Python setuptools ReDoS vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to parse HTML during package lookup; no package lookups occur since all packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin.

GraalVM Python setuptools command injection vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process URLs via download/install functions; those functions are never called at runtime.
